full_response:
Let  

 w₁ , … , wₙ  ( distinct positive numbers )  
 W  ( target weight )

be the input.  
Denote by m the lower-median weight, i.e. the element whose rank is  
⌊(n+1)/2⌋ when the bags are ordered from lightest to heaviest.

Goal Decide in Θ(n) time whether the greedy set Ŝ (the smallest number of
heaviest bags whose total weight is ≥ W) contains the bag of weight m.

-------------------------------------------------
Algorithm
-------------------------------------------------
1. Find m in linear time.  
    Use the deterministic SELECT algorithm (median-of-medians)
    to return the lower median m of the n numbers.

2. Compute the total weight of all bags that are *heavier* than m.  

    S ← 0  
    for each weight w in the input array do  
        if w > m then S ← S + w  

3. Answer  

    if S ≥ W return “NO, m ∉ Ŝ”  
    else   return “YES, m ∈ Ŝ”.

-------------------------------------------------
Proof of correctness
-------------------------------------------------
Order the bags by decreasing weight:

 w(1) > w(2) > … > w(n).

Let k be the number of bags chosen by the greedy algorithm, i.e.

 k = min{ t : ∑_{i=1}^{t} w(i) ≥ W }.
 Ŝ = { w(1), … , w(k) }.

The lower median is w(⌈n/2⌉).  
Define G = { w(1), … , w(⌈n/2⌉–1) },
            S = ∑_{w∈G} w = total weight of all bags heavier than m.

Two exhaustive cases:

1. S ≥ W.  
   After taking at most |G| bags (all heavier than m) the running
   weight is already ≥ W, so k ≤ |G| and the greedy algorithm halts
   *before* reaching m. Hence m ∉ Ŝ.

2. S < W.  
   After every bag heavier than m has been taken the running weight is
   still < W; the algorithm must therefore take the next bag, whose
   weight is exactly m. Thus k ≥ |G|+1 and m ∈ Ŝ.

Therefore  
 m ∈ Ŝ ⇔ S < W,  
and the algorithm returns exactly this predicate, proving correctness.

-------------------------------------------------
Complexity analysis
-------------------------------------------------
• Step 1 (SELECT)         Θ(n) time, Θ(1) extra space.  
• Step 2 (single scan)    Θ(n) time, Θ(1) extra space.  

Total: Θ(n) time, Θ(1) additional space.

\boxed{\text{Pick the lower median }m\text{ in }Θ(n)\text{ time; compute }S=\!\sum_{w>m}w.\;
        m\inŜ\;\text{iff}\;S<W.}

Confidence: 0.74
